other_observers: 1st time: Rachel Hopper, Nick Komar, Joe Roller, Joe Himmel 2nd time: Karl Stecher, Lock Kilpatrick; There were a lot of other birders that saw this bird when I didn't see it. Finder was Dave Leatherman and identified by Joe Mammoser.
specific_location: Grandview Cemetery - Corner of Taft Hill Road and LaPort Avenue.
description: Had the shape of the other warblers, with a short, sharp bill. A yellow breast and throat with a light line of orange on the breast. Pale lower mandible and a dark upper mandible. A dark mask on the face (no white eye ring). Two white wing bars, a blue body and head. A dark under side of the tail. White under tail covert and belly.Behaviors: I the tops of trees fluttering around for a while and then sitting in a single spot that has a lot of foliage. Sometimes it would fly from tree to tree before stopping to rest.Call: a buzzy rising trill ending in a buzzy downslurPlumage: adult
